questionText: What was Nina Simone's original career ambition before music?
correctAnswer: Concert pianist — she wanted to be the first Black female classical pianist
options: Concert pianist — she wanted to be the first Black female classical pianist
funFact: Nina Simone was denied a scholarship to the Curtis Institute of Music — she believed it was because of her race. This rejection radicalized her and shaped her entire career

questionText: Which country is Youssou N'Dour from?
funFact: Youssou N'Dour's '7 Seconds' duet with Neneh Cherry was a worldwide #1 hit — he's also served as Senegal's Minister of Tourism and Culture

questionText: Who is the DJ of The Prodigy?
funFact: Liam Howlett composed all of The Prodigy's music entirely by himself — Keith Flint was the frontman and dancer, not a musician, which made their live shows uniquely explosive

questionText: Which Wes Anderson film features Alexandre Desplat's whimsical score and 'The Grand Budapest Hotel Waltz'?
funFact: Alexandre Desplat composed the score using a combination of Russian balalaika, zither, and alpine instruments — it won the Oscar for Best Original Score

questionText: Which TV series features the iconic 'Peter Gunn Theme' by Henry Mancini?
funFact: The Peter Gunn Theme is considered the first rock and roll TV theme — its driving bass line influenced everyone from The Blues Brothers to Eminem

questionText: Which country is Burna Boy from?
funFact: Burna Boy's grandfather managed Fela Kuti — Afrobeats literally runs in his blood. He won the Grammy for 'Twice as Tall' and headlined Madison Square Garden

questionText: What is Billie Holiday's iconic accessory she always wore while performing?
funFact: Billie Holiday started wearing the gardenia to cover a burn mark from a curling iron — it became so iconic that fans would throw gardenias on stage

questionText: Who was the lead singer of Soundgarden?
funFact: Chris Cornell had a four-octave vocal range — his voice on 'Black Hole Sun' was voted one of the greatest in rock history. He also wrote 'You Know My Name' for James Bond

questionText: Which John Hughes film features 'Don't You (Forget About Me)' by Simple Minds?
funFact: Simple Minds initially rejected the song — they thought it was too pop. After Bryan Ferry and Billy Idol also turned it down, Simple Minds recorded it reluctantly. It became their biggest hit

questionText: Which TV show features 'Suicide is Painless' as its theme?
funFact: The MASH theme was written for the 1970 film — director Robert Altman's 14-year-old son wrote the lyrics because Altman needed them to be 'the stupidest lyrics ever written'

questionText: Which country is Miriam Makeba from?
funFact: Miriam Makeba was exiled from South Africa for 30 years for speaking out against apartheid — Nelson Mandela personally invited her back in 1990

questionText: What was Ray Charles' breakthrough that fused gospel with R&B?
correctAnswer: 'I Got a Woman' (1954) — he put secular lyrics over gospel melodies
options: 'I Got a Woman' (1954) — he put secular lyrics over gospel melodies
funFact: Ray Charles was called a sinner by churches and too holy by clubs — but by combining gospel and blues, he essentially invented soul music

questionText: Who is the guitarist of Rage Against the Machine known for unconventional techniques?
funFact: Tom Morello has a degree from Harvard in Political Science — he uses his guitar as a political weapon, creating sounds with toggle switches and Allen keys instead of effects pedals

questionText: Which Tim Burton film features Danny Elfman's most iconic score?
funFact: Danny Elfman has scored almost every Tim Burton film since Pee-wee's Big Adventure — their partnership has produced some of the most recognizable film music of the last 40 years

questionText: Which crime drama features 'Get Ready' by Rare Earth in its opening credits?
correctAnswer: Brooklyn Nine-Nine does not use this — trick question
options: Brooklyn Nine-Nine does not use this — trick question
funFact: Miami Vice's theme by Jan Hammer is one of the few TV themes to reach #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 — it defined the sound of 1980s television

questionText: Which country is Sean Paul from?
funFact: Sean Paul was a competitive water polo player who represented Jamaica internationally before his music career took off with 'Get Busy'

questionText: What concept album tells the story of a rock opera about a pinball wizard?
funFact: Pete Townshend wrote Tommy while following the teachings of Meher Baba, an Indian spiritual master — the opera's themes of spiritual awakening reflect this influence

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Bon Jovi?
funFact: Jon Bon Jovi's real name is John Francis Bongiovi Jr. — he worked as a janitor at his cousin's recording studio before 'Livin' on a Prayer' made him a superstar

questionText: Which film features 'Moon River' performed by Audrey Hepburn?
funFact: The studio wanted to cut 'Moon River' from the film — Audrey Hepburn reportedly said 'Over my dead body!' It went on to win the Oscar for Best Original Song

questionText: Which TV show features 'Bad Reputation' by Joan Jett?
funFact: Joan Jett was rejected by 23 record labels before starting her own — Blackheart Records became one of the first successful female-owned labels in rock history

questionText: Which country is Rachmaninoff from?
funFact: Rachmaninoff had extraordinarily large hands — he could span 12 piano keys, which is why some of his pieces are nearly impossible for pianists with normal-sized hands

questionText: What was Ella Fitzgerald's signature vocal technique?
funFact: Ella Fitzgerald originally auditioned as a dancer at the Apollo Theater — she was too nervous to dance, so she sang instead and won the amateur contest

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Iron Maiden?
funFact: Bruce Dickinson is also a licensed commercial airline pilot — he personally flew the band's Boeing 747 'Ed Force One' on world tours

questionText: Which Ridley Scott film features Vangelis' iconic electronic score?
funFact: The Blade Runner score wasn't officially released for 12 years due to legal disputes — bootleg copies became some of the most sought-after recordings in film music history

questionText: Which TV drama features 'You Only Live Twice' reworked by Björk?
correctAnswer: None — but Björk performed a Bond-style theme for the film Dancer in the Dark
options: None — but Björk performed a Bond-style theme for the film Dancer in the Dark
funFact: Björk won the Best Actress award at Cannes for Dancer in the Dark — the only time she's acted in a film, and she said the experience was so traumatic she'll never act again

questionText: Which country is Susumu Hirasawa from?
funFact: Hirasawa's music for the anime Berserk and film Paprika has influenced countless Western artists — Christopher Nolan reportedly watched Paprika before making Inception

questionText: What is the longest song ever to reach #1 on the Billboard Hot 100?
correctAnswer: All Too Well (10 Minute Version) by Taylor Swift
options: All Too Well (10 Minute Version) by Taylor Swift
November Rain by Guns N' Roses
funFact: At 10 minutes 13 seconds, 'All Too Well (Taylor's Version)' shattered the record — the previous holder 'American Pie' was 8 minutes 42 seconds and had held the record since 1972

questionText: Who was the lead singer of Motörhead?
funFact: Lemmy drank a bottle of Jack Daniel's daily for decades and lived to 70 — when asked about health advice he said 'I don't advise it for anyone else. I just got lucky with the genes'

questionText: Which Christopher Nolan film features a ticking clock sound throughout Hans Zimmer's score?
funFact: Zimmer used recordings of Nolan's personal pocket watch for the ticking — it creates constant tension. The entire score is built around three interlocking timelines at different tempos

questionText: Which animated series features a jazz theme performed by Yoko Kanno?
funFact: Yoko Kanno composed in nearly every genre for Cowboy Bebop — jazz, blues, heavy metal, country, opera — making it the most musically diverse anime soundtrack ever

questionText: Which country is Aphex Twin from?
funFact: Aphex Twin (Richard D. James) grew up in Cornwall and reportedly slept only 2-3 hours per night — he claims to compose music in his lucid dreams

questionText: Which classical composer went completely deaf but continued composing?
funFact: Beethoven composed his 9th Symphony — including the 'Ode to Joy' — while completely deaf. At the premiere, he had to be turned around to see the audience's standing ovation because he couldn't hear it

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Massive Attack?
funFact: Robert Del Naja is also a famous graffiti artist — there's a persistent internet theory (never confirmed) that he is actually Banksy

questionText: Which film features 'Gonna Fly Now' as its training montage theme?
funFact: 'Gonna Fly Now' was composed in one day by Bill Conti — the running-up-the-steps scene was filmed guerilla-style on the streets of Philadelphia with no permits

questionText: Which country is Tinariwen from?
funFact: Tinariwen formed in a Libyan military camp in the 1980s among Tuareg rebels — their music is literally a soundtrack to revolution

questionText: Which TV series features a baroque harpsichord theme composed by Nathan Barr?
options: What We Do in the Shadows
funFact: The True Blood theme 'Bad Things' by Jace Everett was actually released 3 years before the show — HBO found it and thought it perfectly captured Southern Gothic vampire vibes

questionText: What distinction does Missy Elliott hold in the music industry?
correctAnswer: First female hip-hop artist inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
options: First female hip-hop artist inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
First rapper to win an Oscar
First female producer to go platinum
funFact: Missy Elliott and Timbaland created some of the most innovative music videos ever — 'Supa Dupa Fly' garbage bag outfit is one of the most iconic images in hip-hop

questionText: Which country is Nephew from?
funFact: Nephew are from Aarhus and sing primarily in Danish — their song 'Igen & Igen & Igen' is one of the most played Danish rock songs of the 2000s

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Tears for Fears?
correctAnswer: Curt Smith and Roland Orzabal (both sing lead)
options: Curt Smith and Roland Orzabal (both sing lead)
funFact: 'Everybody Wants to Rule the World' was nearly left off the album — Roland Orzabal thought it was too pop. It became their biggest hit and one of the definitive songs of the 1980s
backgroundSongName: Everybody Wants to Rule the World

questionText: Which 2017 horror film features a score that uses only a cello?
funFact: Get Out's score by Michael Abels mixes Swahili chanting with strings — the opening theme translates to 'run, listen to your ancestors, run' which is literally the film's message

questionText: Which country is Ryuichi Sakamoto from?
funFact: Sakamoto won the Oscar for his 'Merry Christmas, Mr. Lawrence' score and later composed for 'The Revenant' — he was equally revered in electronic music as a member of Yellow Magic Orchestra

questionText: What famous music festival took place on Max Yasgur's dairy farm in 1969?
funFact: Hendrix played the Star-Spangled Banner at Woodstock at 8 AM Monday morning — most of the 400,000 attendees had already left. The remaining crowd witnessed one of music's most iconic moments

questionText: Which Danish comedy-drama features music by Halfdan E?
funFact: Klovn (Clown) became Denmark's answer to Curb Your Enthusiasm — Halfdan E's scores for Danish film and TV have made him one of the most prolific Danish composers

questionText: Which country is Nicki Minaj from?
funFact: Nicki Minaj moved from Trinidad to Queens, New York at age 5 — she's the best-selling female rapper of all time with over 100 million records sold

questionText: Which Motown artist was known as 'The Prince of Motown'?
funFact: Marvin Gaye's 'What's Going On' was initially rejected by Motown boss Berry Gordy who called it 'the worst thing I've ever heard' — it became one of the greatest albums ever made

questionText: Who is the vocalist of Florence + The Machine?
funFact: Florence Welch has synesthesia — she sees colors when she hears music. She described 'Dog Days Are Over' as 'bright orange and gold'

questionText: Which Spike Lee film features Public Enemy's 'Fight the Power'?
funFact: Spike Lee asked Public Enemy to write a song as powerful as the protest anthems of the 1960s — 'Fight the Power' became one of hip-hop's greatest political statements

questionText: Which Netflix sci-fi series features a score by Ben Salisbury and Geoff Barrow of Portishead?
funFact: Geoff Barrow went from creating Portishead's trip-hop masterpiece 'Dummy' to scoring dystopian TV — proving that genre boundaries are meaningless for truly creative musicians
